body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: The teaching quality in my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication has been exceptional this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly commend the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The curriculum for my Public Relations program at the School of Communication is well-designed and clearly aligned with current industry demands and emerging professional trends in the field. Course structures are logical, building progressively on foundational concepts before introducing advanced topics, which gives students a solid intellectual scaffold. The balance between theoretical foundations and practical application is impressive, with project-based assessments, case studies, and laboratory work that directly reflect real-world professional scenarios I will encounter in my career. I particularly value the inclusion of contemporary tools and technologies, which ensures graduates are competitive and well-prepared for the demands of the modern job market.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: The assessment methods employed in my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication are outstanding and demonstrate a thoughtful approach to measuring genuine student understanding. The balanced mix of individual assignments, group projects, presentations, and written examinations ensures that different learning styles and competencies are recognized and fairly evaluated throughout the semester. Rubrics are consistently shared in advance, assessment criteria are transparent, and feedback is provided promptly enough to inform improvement in subsequent tasks. I particularly applaud the emphasis on applied problem-solving assessments rather than rote memorization, which I believe is a much stronger indicator of actual competency and readiness for professional practice.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer availability at the School of Communication for my Public Relations courses is moderate, with noticeable differences between individual faculty members in their accessibility and responsiveness to student needs outside of scheduled class sessions. A number of lecturers are readily available and proactive in offering support, which I find invaluable during intensive assignment and examination periods. Others, however, have limited and poorly communicated office hours, making it challenging to get timely guidance on coursework challenges. Establishing a standardized minimum availability requirement and publishing these schedules on the student portal would greatly improve accessibility for all students.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: Learning resources for my Public Relations program at the School of Communication are adequate, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is exceptional and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Communication this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I applaud the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: The academic administrative processes at the School of Communication, overseen by the Academic Affairs Office, have been a source of considerable frustration for many students this semester. Course registration was plagued by system errors and conflicting timetables that took weeks to resolve, causing students to miss early enrollment advantages. Grade dispute processes are opaque and slow, with students waiting over a month without updates or acknowledgment of their submissions. Communication from administrative offices is reactive rather than proactive, and staff sometimes provide inconsistent information depending on who is contacted. I highlight a comprehensive review of administrative workflows, with investment in both system improvements and staff training as a matter of urgency.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been impressive and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also acknowledge the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: Campus facilities at the School of Communication, maintained by the Facilities Management, are generally decent and adequate for day-to-day academic activities, though several areas require attention and investment to meet growing needs. Core academic spaces — including lecture halls and laboratories — are functional and reasonably maintained, though some equipment is beginning to show age and inconsistency in performance. Student study and relaxation spaces are insufficient during peak periods, and the condition of older campus buildings varies considerably. A systematic facilities audit followed by a prioritized improvement plan would help address longstanding issues and ensure the campus environment supports the academic ambitions of all students more effectively.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: My key recommendation for the School of Communication is to urgently address the inconsistent systemic issues that have affected Public Relations students this semester, rather than treating them as isolated incidents. A structured, transparent student feedback mechanism — where concerns are acknowledged within a set timeframe and outcomes communicated back to students — would significantly rebuild trust that has been eroded by unaddressed issues. I also strongly highlight leadership to conduct a comprehensive curriculum and service quality review in genuine consultation with current students, alumni, and industry partners. Without meaningful action on the concerns raised in this feedback, student satisfaction and the institution's competitive standing will continue to decline in the years ahead.
